suggestions.rationale: Deferred from ACDE #232 with explicit March 26 deadline. Requires Besu, Nethermind, or Reth support to proceed; default is rejection without additional client backing. This is the last scheduled opportunity for Hegotá headliner selection.

Critical blocker for bal-devnet-3 launch. March 19 repricing breakout was scheduled to resolve complexity issues with 8037/7928/7778 alignment and mempool rules. Devnet-3 has been delayed pending this resolution since early March.

Devnet-3 blocked since ACDE #232 due to EIP-8037 complexity and low client readiness. With repricing breakout scheduled for March 19, clients need to confirm implementation status and launch readiness post-breakout resolution.

ACDE #232 noted prefetch optimization was slower than expected. BAL optimizations were top priority for February deadline. Teams should share final benchmarking data and decide whether to adjust optimization strategy or repricing assumptions.

CFI'd for Glamsterdam in ACDE #230 and included in devnet-3 spec as optional. nft-devnet-10 was testing ETH/70 P2P protocol. Client teams should confirm implementation progress and whether these remain optional or become required for devnet-3.

Remained CFI in ACDE #229 but with unresolved implementation divergence across clients. No updates since January 30. Should either resolve divergence or reconsider CFI status before Glamsterdam spec finalization.

Running for ~2 weeks as of ACDE #232 with Lighthouse/Lodestar/Prysm working, Teku/Nimbus joining. Exits and builders not working. Update on builder integration progress and whether this affects Hegotá timeline planning.

ACDE #232 highlighted DAO migration timeline 2028-2029 requiring native AA soon. With headliner selection concluding, discuss how PQ urgency affects Hegotá vs I-Star feature prioritization and fork scheduling.
